questions.question: Explain the relationship between arrays and pointers in C with an example.
keyPoints: Array name is a constant pointer to the first element
arr[i] is equivalent to *(arr + i)
Both arr and &arr[0] give the same address but different types
Pointer arithmetic works with arrays

questions.question: Write a C program to dynamically allocate memory for an array of n integers and find their sum.
keyPoints: Use malloc() to allocate n * sizeof(int) bytes
Check for NULL after malloc
Accept n from user using scanf
Loop through array to calculate sum
Free allocated memory

questions.question: What is the difference between int *ptr[5] and int (*ptr)[5]? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: int *ptr[5] - array of 5 integer pointers
int (*ptr)[5] - pointer to array of 5 integers
Parentheses change precedence and meaning
Show memory layout for both

questions.question: Explain pointer arithmetic in C with an example showing addition and subtraction operations.
keyPoints: Pointer can be incremented/decremented
Adding integer n moves pointer by n * sizeof(type)
Subtracting two pointers gives number of elements between them
Multiplication and division of pointers not allowed

questions.question: What are malloc(), calloc(), and free() functions? Explain their differences.
keyPoints: malloc() allocates specified bytes, memory uninitialized
calloc() allocates for n elements, initialized to zero
free() deallocates previously allocated memory
calloc(n, size) = malloc(n*size) but with zero initialization

questions.question: Explain the concept of NULL pointer and void pointer in C with use cases.
keyPoints: NULL pointer points to invalid memory address (0)
Used to initialize pointers before assignment
void pointer (void*) can point to any type
Must cast void pointer before dereferencing

questions.question: Write a C program to reverse an array using pointers.
keyPoints: Use two pointers - one at start, one at end
Swap elements using pointers
Move pointers towards center
Continue until pointers meet

questions.question: Write a complete C program to create a dynamic 2D matrix (m x n) using pointer-to-pointer approach. Include functions to input elements, display the matrix, and free the allocated memory.
keyPoints: First allocate array of row pointers using malloc(m * sizeof(int*))
Then allocate each row using malloc(n * sizeof(int))
Use nested loops for input and display
Free each row first, then free array of pointers
Handle edge cases like m=0 or n=0
Check for NULL after each malloc

questions.question: Explain how arrays are passed to functions in C. Discuss pass-by-reference semantics, array decay, and how the function parameter declaration affects the received pointer.
keyPoints: Arrays passed to functions decay to pointers
Function receives pointer to first element
Parameter int arr[] and int *arr are equivalent
Size specification in parameter is ignored
Changes to array elements affect original array
Use sizeof on parameter gives pointer size, not array size

questions.question: Discuss memory allocation in C with emphasis on static vs dynamic allocation. Explain malloc, calloc, realloc, and free with their purposes, differences, and common pitfalls like memory leaks and dangling pointers.
keyPoints: Static - compile-time allocation (arrays with fixed size)
Dynamic - runtime allocation using heap memory
malloc: raw allocation, calloc: zero-initialized
realloc: resize existing allocation
free: release memory back to heap
Memory leak - forgetting to free
Dangling pointer - accessing freed memory
Always check NULL return from malloc/calloc/realloc

questions.question: Explain the differences betweenarr, &arr, *arr, arr[0], &arr[0], and *(&arr[0]). Show what each evaluates to and their types with example int arr[5];
keyPoints: arr and &arr[0] - address of first element (int*)
&arr - address of entire array (int(*)[5])
*arr and arr[0] - value at first element
*(&arr[0]) - also value at first element
All may have same numeric value but different types
sizeof(arr) vs sizeof(ptr) demonstrates difference
